label: H1B / US Visa Crisis
subtitle: Indian nationals facing visa status threat in the United States
disclaimer: Informational resource only. NOT legal advice. Consult a licensed US immigration attorney for your specific situation. Laws and policies change rapidly - verify everything independently.

levels.label: GREEN
levels.name: Normal
levels.description: Visa valid, employment stable, no active policy threat.
levels.officialTrigger: No USCIS policy changes announced. Employer in good standing. Visa validity 18+ months.
levels.observableTrigger: Workplace normal. No RFE received. No significant visa-related news in your category.

levels.label: YELLOW
levels.name: Watch
levels.description: Policy uncertainty elevated. No direct threat yet. Time to prepare - use it.
levels.officialTrigger: USCIS announces policy review affecting H1B. Executive orders signed. Increased RFE rates reported industry-wide.
levels.observableTrigger: Layoffs in your sector. Colleagues receiving RFEs or denials. Immigration attorneys reporting increased caseloads.

levels.label: ORANGE
levels.name: Advisory
levels.description: Direct threat to your status. RFE received, layoff notice, or policy change directly affecting you.
levels.officialTrigger: RFE received. Layoff notice with visa implications. USCIS denial of extension filed.
levels.observableTrigger: HR discussing visa sponsorship limits. Project cancelled. Team downsizing announced.

nodes.label:
New sponsor
or status path
identified?

MANAGING
Stay on attorney
timeline strictly

Retain attorney
immediately
checklist: Do not respond to any USCIS notice without attorney review - ever
File RFE response strictly within deadline - no extensions exist
Explore in parallel: change of status, transfer to new employer, COS to F-1

nodes.label:
60-day clock
management
checklist: H1B holders have exactly 60 days after layoff before status lapses
Do NOT accrue unlawful presence - consequences are a 3 or 10 year bar on re-entry
Options within 60 days: new H1B sponsor, change to O-1, H4, F-1, or voluntary departure
Attorney should map your specific timeline on day one

nodes.label:
Parallel path
preparation
checklist: Begin job search immediately - do not wait for RFE outcome
Identify O-1 eligibility: extraordinary ability track, different from H1B
If spouse has H1B: explore H4 EAD path
Begin India-side preparation in parallel: update resume, reconnect with India contacts

levels.label: RED
levels.name: Warning
levels.description: Status lapsing or lapsed. Departure timeline active. No viable status path remaining.
levels.officialTrigger: USCIS denial final. Status expired. Attorney confirms no further remedies.
levels.observableTrigger: Out of status. Employer cannot or will not sponsor. No transfer candidate.

nodes.label:
LEGAL PROCESS
Follow attorney
timeline strictly

DEPARTING
Voluntary departure
is far better
than removal order

Last legal
options
checklist: Appeal USCIS denial - strict deadlines, attorney required
Motion to reopen or reconsider - narrow grounds but worth assessing
Consular processing from India - may allow re-entry on approved petition
Emergency stay of removal if applicable - attorney only

nodes.label:
Voluntary departure
preparation
checklist: Voluntary departure avoids the 3-year or 10-year re-entry bar that a removal order triggers
Settle US accounts: close or transfer credit cards, redirect mail, notify IRS of address change
Roll 401k to IRA to avoid forced withdrawal and penalties - do not just cash out
Wire savings to India: daily wire limits apply, plan over several days
Contact Indian Consulate for any re-entry documentation needed
Do not leave without informing your attorney - even voluntary departure has a process

levels.label: BLACK
levels.name: Emergency
levels.description: Removal proceedings active. Enforcement contact made. Immediate legal and personal crisis.
levels.officialTrigger: Removal order issued. Notice to Appear. ICE contact or detention.
levels.observableTrigger: Enforcement officers present. Employer received inquiry about your status.

nodes.label:
WITH ATTORNEY
Do not act
without counsel
under any
circumstance

Immediate steps
in this order only
checklist: Do not speak to enforcement officers without an attorney present - this is a legal right, exercise it
You have the right to remain silent. State clearly: I wish to speak to an attorney.
You have the right to consular access. State: I am an Indian citizen and request consular notification.
Call your immigration attorney immediately - this is the one call you make first
Contact Indian Consulate: they can provide consular access during detention and contact your family
Do not sign any documents without attorney review - ever, under any pressure
